steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to emphasize the historical significance of Stephen Amos’s enlistment date. The decisive clue in the notes is that only a small number of Filipino-descent sailors served in the Civil War at all — placing Amos within that rare group is what makes his date significant.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Amos enlisted… two years later than Ignases’: This compares Amos’s date to Ignases’s date but never explains why either date matters historically — later does not mean significant. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘along with Raphael Ignases, who was born… around 1834’: This mentions Ignases’s birth year instead of Amos’s enlistment date and provides no connection to the Civil War’s historical context. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘making him one of the few sailors of Filipino descent to serve in the US Civil War (1861–1865)’: This gives the enlistment date and directly connects it to the rarity established by the notes — exactly the historical significance the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘he joined sailors such as Raphael Ignases, who had been born in the Philippines’: This provides Amos’s enlistment date but frames it only as joining another sailor, not as belonging to a historically rare group. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
